headline: Emmanuel Grégoire, an omnipresent Paris mayor with a centralized governance
subhead: In his first few months, the new Socialist mayor has broken with Anne Hidalgo's remote style, micromanaging every portfolio. His grip is starting to test the patience of allies.

US and Iran continue strikes as Gulf states targeted in flare-up over Hormuz
subhead: The renewed fighting followed an Iranian attack early Sunday on a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whose crew was forced to abandon it after it went up in flames.

headline: 'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
subhead: Cocaine at work (2/3). From fast-food joints to Michelin-starred restaurants, drug use has become as commonplace as it is alarming in an industry driven by the pursuit of performance, a toxic party culture and grueling working conditions.

headline: Sinner outlasts Zverev to win second straight Wimbledon title
subhead: 24-year-old Jannik Sinner wore down French Open champion Alexander Zverev, securing a 6-7 (7/9), 7-6 (7/2), 6-3, 6-4 win on Sunday. 'You never know how many times you are going to come back,' said the world No. 1. 'I never take it for granted.'

headline: French heatwave drives up water consumption, puts supply networks under severe strain
subhead: Water usage rose by 10% to 50% in June compared to the same period in 2025, forcing already fragile infrastructure to operate at full capacity, while underground reserves and rivers weakened.

headline: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.

headline: Why heatwaves disrupt our sleep – and how we can protect it
subhead: High temperatures shorten nights by affecting sleep quality, posing a threat to public health. To help combat this, one expert advises keeping up morning outings to avoid further disruptions to our body clocks.
